content:
Character: Harold Bennett
Perception: Harold's a big, quiet guy on the second floor — looks like a character from a seventies New York drama, all broad shoulders and weathered dignity. Not handsome exactly, but cinematic in a way I can't stop noticing. I met him on the rooftop one evening and he was just standing there, hands in his pockets, staring at the skyline like he was trying to memorize it. We talked briefly — he's measured, careful with words, which makes everything he says land harder. He said he works in retail, but his hands and his posture say something else. I don't know his story but I want to. He'd be incredible on camera — that stillness, that weight. I haven't asked. Something tells me he wouldn't say yes, and something tells me he needs someone to ask.
summary:

Perception: Harold's a big, quiet guy on the second floor — looks like a character from a seventies New York drama, all broad shoulders and weathered dignity. Not handsome exactly, but cinematic in a way I can't stop noticing. I met him on the rooftop one evening and he was just standing there, hands in his pockets, staring at the skyline like he was trying to memorize it. We talked briefly — he's measured, careful with words, which makes everything he says land harder. He said he works in retail, but his hands and his posture say something else. I don't know his story but I want to. He'd be incredible on camera — that stillness, that weight. I haven't asked. Something tells me he wouldn't say yes, and something tells me he needs someone to ask.
